$comment: D-CSUBSET-FN-TYPEDEF-PROTOTYPE: a `T x;` declaration where T is a function-TYPE typedef declares x as a function PROTOTYPE (C 6.7 / 6.9.1p2) — the shape of SQLite test_thread.c's `static Tcl_ObjCmdProc sqlthread_proc;`. `static Fn apply; static Fn twice;` are prototypes forward-called in main, then defined below; they MERGE with their definitions (internal linkage). Value-divergent: apply(20)=21, twice(21)=42 -> exit 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the Pass-1 maybeFnTypedefProto candidate + the Pass-1.5 FnSig upgrade -> the bare `static Fn apply;` is minted an OBJECT -> S0018 at the prototype + S0002 at the definition -> the program no longer COMPILES (compile failure, not exit 42). The release arm re-witnesses under the shipped pipeline (two ordinary functions; no proto node survives lowering).
